response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings impressive technical depth and breadth across 20 years of software development, with genuine developer tooling contributions (test frameworks, CI infrastructure, MCP tooling). However, the role is fundamentally a people management position requiring demonstrated experience leading, growing, and evaluating engineering teams — an area entirely absent from his resume. All titles are individual contributor tracks (Software Developer, Senior, Staff), with no evidence of direct reports, performance management, organizational change leadership, or team-level roadmap ownership. The technical foundation is solid, but the management gap is too significant for a mid-to-senior Engineering Manager role.
strengths: 20+ years of hands-on software development across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — well above the 8-year minimum
Genuine developer tooling experience: built open-source test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), set up CI/CD infrastructure, and currently contributes to MCP code generators and agentic frameworks
Broad technical credibility with a track record of public speaking, teaching, and community contributions (workshops, open-source projects, conference talks)
concerns: Zero evidence of people management: no direct reports, no performance reviews, no hiring or career development responsibilities mentioned anywhere in a 20-year career
No exposure to large-scale mobile platform ecosystems, build systems, or the kind of organizational-scale developer productivity work the role centers on

Entire career is on the individual contributor track; the jump to managing an engineering productivity team at mid-to-senior management level (Level II Manager) would be a significant and unsupported leap

key_factors.factor: No large-scale mobile platform or build systems experience
